# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
  agent any

  options {
    timestamps()
    disableConcurrentBuilds()
  }

  parameters {
    string(name: 'REGISTRY_URL', defaultValue: 'registry.example.com', description: 'Target Docker registry hostname (and optional namespace), e.g. registry.example.com/team')
    string(name: 'IMAGE_NAME', defaultValue: 'my-app', description: 'Docker image name (without registry), e.g. my-app')
    string(name: 'IMAGE_TAG', defaultValue: 'latest', description: 'Docker image tag, e.g. 1.2.3')
    string(name: 'DOCKERFILE', defaultValue: 'Dockerfile', description: 'Path to Dockerfile')
    string(name: 'CONTEXT_DIR', defaultValue: '.', description: 'Docker build context directory')
  }

  environment {
    FULL_IMAGE = "${params.REGISTRY_URL}/${params.IMAGE_NAME}:${params.IMAGE_TAG}"
  }

  stages {
    stage('Checkout') {
      steps {
        checkout scm
      }
    }

    stage('Build Image') {
      steps {
        sh """
          set -euo pipefail
          echo "Building: ${FULL_IMAGE}"
          docker build -f "${params.DOCKERFILE}" -t "${FULL_IMAGE}" "${params.CONTEXT_DIR}"
        """
      }
    }

    stage('Push Image') {
      steps {
        sh """
          set -euo pipefail
          echo "Pushing: ${FULL_IMAGE}"
          docker push "${FULL_IMAGE}"
        """
      }
    }
  }

  post {
    always {
      sh 'docker image ls | head -n 50 || true'
    }
  }
}
